jokes.text: Oh, yeah? Well, track this motion! [Fry gives the X-Cube camera the finger]
jokes.setup: X-Cube tracks motions with built-in camera for controller-free gaming
jokes.punchline: Fry responds by flipping off the camera
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Fry - juvenile response to technology. Simple setup with immediate visual payoff.

jokes.text: Sounds like fun on a bun!
jokes.setup: Bender invites them to play video games
jokes.punchline: Fry's nonsensical enthusiastic catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Typical Fry nonsense phrase that fits his character but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Sorry to kill und run, but I really must be Göring.
jokes.setup: German video game character kills Fry and Bender
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'going' using Nazi Hermann Göring's name
jokes.explanation: Clever historical pun that works on multiple levels - 'going' pun plus Nazi reference fits German character context.

jokes.text: Pwned again!
jokes.setup: They get defeated in video games again
jokes.punchline: Fry uses gaming slang 'pwned'
jokes.explanation: Dated internet slang reference with minimal comedic impact.

jokes.text: You can't even keep up with me, and I'm some sort of Stone Age throwback.
jokes.setup: Bender complaining about his outdated circuitry
jokes.punchline: Fry points out he's even slower despite being a primitive human
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor that plays into Fry's fish-out-of-water status and his gaming ineptitude.

jokes.text: You say it'll put some whoopie in my cushion?
jokes.setup: Cubert offers to overclock Bender to improve his reflexes
jokes.punchline: Bender mishears/interprets it as sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender sexual innuendo that fits his character but is fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Sounds like fun on a bun! - Deleted. - Aw.
jokes.setup: Cubert is deleting Bender's outdated catchphrases while overclocking him
jokes.punchline: Bender says the catchphrase just as it gets deleted
jokes.explanation: Excellent timing gag that callbacks to earlier moment while showing the overclocking process.

jokes.text: Sure, but you're always in it. Also, sometimes terminators.
jokes.setup: Leela asks if Fry ever wonders about the future
jokes.punchline: Sweet response followed by random Terminator reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - sweet sentiment undercut by his pop culture-addled brain. Great economy and character work.

jokes.text: Ooh, I'm smart! I know how to spell 'aardvark'! February is the shortest month! There's... 3,018 jelly beans in that jar! Oh, damn, I'm good! I mean 3,018 rat kidneys.
jokes.setup: Bender has been overclocked and is showing off his enhanced intelligence
jokes.punchline: Escalating from simple facts to impossible precision, then dark twist about rat kidneys
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from mundane to absurd with perfect Bender darkness at the end. Good structure building to punchline.

jokes.text: Hey, foreign aggressors, wassup?
jokes.setup: WWII-themed video game with Nazi opponents
jokes.punchline: Bender casually greets enemies with modern slang
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between serious war setting and Bender's casual modern attitude.

jokes.text: It's my brains against your Von Braun!
jokes.setup: Germans fire V-2 rocket at Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender makes pun on 'Von Braun' (rocket scientist) vs 'brawn'
jokes.explanation: Clever historical pun that works on multiple levels - brain/brawn wordplay plus actual rocket scientist reference. Smart writing.

jokes.text: Ach! I could have fired a V8!
jokes.setup: German player frustrated at missing Bender with V-2 rocket
jokes.punchline: References V8 vegetable juice instead of more powerful rocket
jokes.explanation: Decent pun playing on V-series rockets vs V8 drink, but feels like a stretch.

jokes.text: Mommy! What is it, you colicky bastards!
jokes.setup: Mom's adult sons run to her crying about losing at video games
jokes.punchline: Mom's harsh response to her grown children
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Mom - maternal but abusive. Good contrast between childish behavior and harsh response.

jokes.text: Nobody rips off my kids but me!
jokes.setup: Mom's sons complain about being cheated at video games
jokes.punchline: Mom's twisted protective instinct
jokes.explanation: Excellent character line that perfectly captures Mom's twisted maternal nature. Great economy and dark humor.

jokes.text: It better not be those little Korean girls again.
jokes.setup: Mom investigating who beat her sons at video games
jokes.punchline: Reference to stereotypically skilled Korean gamers
jokes.explanation: Relies on gaming stereotype for humor. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: That nerdburglar overclocked the robot's processor!
jokes.setup: Mom discovers Cubert modified Bender
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'nerdburglar' combining 'nerd' and 'burglar'
jokes.explanation: Creative neologism that fits Mom's villainous vocabulary. Good word construction.

jokes.text: Even an idiot like me knows that. - An idiot like you knows nothing! - Aah!
jokes.setup: Walt tries to contribute to conversation
jokes.punchline: Mom's harsh put-down and Walt's scared reaction
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing Mom's abuse of her sons. Pattern will repeat later.

jokes.text: Ding-dong. I'm saying 'ding-dong' 'cause you don't have a doorbell.
jokes.setup: Police robot URL arrives at Planet Express
jokes.punchline: URL explains his verbal doorbell sound
jokes.explanation: Perfect robotic literalism that fits URL's character. Self-aware explanation makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You're porkin' at him.
jokes.setup: Police ask to speak to Cubert J. Farnsworth
jokes.punchline: URL's pig-themed speech pattern: 'porkin'' instead of 'looking'
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency with URL's pig references. Will be repeated for running gag effect.

jokes.text: Your porkin' at him.
jokes.setup: Asked who Cubert's legal guardian is
jokes.punchline: URL repeats his pig-speak pattern
jokes.explanation: Running gag repetition that reinforces URL's character quirk. Less impact than first use.

jokes.text: Even an idiot like me knows he'll be ruined. - An idiot like you is correct! - Aah!
jokes.setup: Walt comments on Farnsworth's legal trouble
jokes.punchline: Mom's backhanded agreement and Walt's fear
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that shows consistency in Mom's abusive treatment. Good running gag.

jokes.text: How. You taught me how.
jokes.setup: Professor asks what he taught Cubert about tinkering with machinery
jokes.punchline: Cubert's matter-of-fact response
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery and economy. Single word punchline that subverts Professor's expectation.

jokes.text: I really shouldn't agree to things I don't understand, but I'm slightly thirsty.
jokes.setup: Bender needs to click license agreement to bend a straw
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges the problem but does it anyway for trivial reason
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on modern digital life and user agreements. Relatable premise with good character logic.

jokes.text: Oh, God! I clicked without reading! And I slightly modified a thing that I own! We're monsters!
jokes.setup: Bender realizes he agreed to license terms and modified himself
jokes.punchline: Dramatic overreaction to minor infractions
jokes.explanation: Good satire of digital paranoia and terms of service hysteria. Nice escalation to melodrama.

jokes.text: Can you die happy now? - Not yet. - Darn.
jokes.setup: Walt asks if Mom can die happy now that she's caught Farnsworth
jokes.punchline: Mom's honest answer and Walt's disappointed response
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Walt's genuine concern and Mom's never-satisfied nature.

jokes.text: Good news, everyone! That's what the professor would say if weren't in jail facing a life sentence.
jokes.setup: Hermes starts with Professor's catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Immediately explains why he can't finish it properly
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke that acknowledges the show's own formula while advancing the plot.

jokes.text: You a big dummy, Einstein! Get a haircut!
jokes.setup: Bender describing how smart he'll be with extra processor
jokes.punchline: Imagines insulting Einstein with juvenile taunts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - even super-intelligent, he'd still be crude and juvenile. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: What are all these pagey things?
jokes.setup: Smart Bender looking at books
jokes.punchline: Calls books 'pagey things' in mock-ignorant way
jokes.explanation: Shows Bender's newfound intelligence through condescending fake-ignorance about books.

jokes.text: I can anticipate that the ceiling fan's gonna fall and knock Zoidberg unconscious. Wrong, Mr. Genius. Not that ceiling fan. Aah!
jokes.setup: Bender predicts ceiling fan will fall on Zoidberg
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg smugly corrects him, then different fan falls on him
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect setup and payoff. Zoidberg's smugness makes the reversal funnier.

jokes.text: Who's up for a turkey dinner and a game of badminton? Say what?
jokes.setup: Bender in middle of serious conversation about Mom
jokes.punchline: Suddenly suggests wholesome family activities
jokes.explanation: Random shift suggests Bender's overheating is affecting his logic. Moderately effective non sequitur.

jokes.text: I hear turkey. Hey, hey, hey. Hey. Hey, hey. Hey.
jokes.setup: Bender responding to Mom's officers coming to arrest him
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by turkey mention, then presumably dancing/fighting
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that follows up the turkey non sequitur. Relies on physical comedy not visible in transcript.

jokes.text: I can conceive of gravies that would boggle your tiny mind!
jokes.setup: Someone asks about gravy for the turkey dinner
jokes.punchline: Super-intelligent Bender applies his enhanced intellect to gravy
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor applying cosmic intelligence to mundane cooking. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Farewell, monobrains.
jokes.setup: Enhanced Bender leaving his friends behind
jokes.punchline: Condescending term for single-brained humans
jokes.explanation: Clever neologism that shows Bender's growing superiority complex. Great economy.

jokes.text: That's my name.
jokes.setup: Lawyer dramatically says 'this boy, this Cubert Farnsworth'
jokes.punchline: Cubert's matter-of-fact confirmation
jokes.explanation: Simple deflation of lawyer's dramatic rhetoric. Good comedic timing.

jokes.text: We don't know where the hell he is. Your Honor, that is something we cannot a-doodle-doo.
jokes.setup: Judge asks to produce Bender for trial
jokes.punchline: Judge's folksy expression combining legal speak with rooster sounds
jokes.explanation: Typical Futurama judge character with folksy nonsense expressions. Moderately effective.

jokes.text: ... four fingers.
jokes.setup: Professor calculating how long until bankruptcy at $10,000/day
jokes.punchline: Shows four fingers for his calculation result
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag showing Professor's literal-mindedness and Planet Express's financial situation.

jokes.text: If we could mount one big musical featuring everyone's talents, we might just save Planet Express! We'll call it Nibbler on the Roof!
jokes.setup: Amy suggests saving the company with a musical
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'Fiddler on the Roof' with Nibbler
jokes.explanation: Standard musical theater reference with character substitution. Predictable sitcom trope.

jokes.text: hearing the words Nibbler on the Roof has given me the kick in the pants I needed.
jokes.setup: Amy's terrible musical idea
jokes.punchline: Leela claims it inspired her to leave
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - the terrible idea motivates Leela to finally leave. Nice callback structure.

jokes.text: Stay strong, Fry. You're the best friend I have left. And I barely even know you.
jokes.setup: Randy trying to comfort Fry about his problems
jokes.punchline: Reveals their friendship is barely existent
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor about superficial friendships. Effective escalation from supportive to pathetic.

jokes.text: What are you, Randy, a complete moron? I'd love to talk to Bender, but I have no idea where he is.
jokes.setup: Randy suggests asking Bender for help
jokes.punchline: Fry points out the obvious problem with this suggestion
jokes.explanation: Typical frustrated response that sets up Randy's actually helpful suggestion. Functional setup.

jokes.text: Oh, you're no help at all! I'm gonna go kill myself. Wait a second. Power? Liquid coolant? That gives me the option of electrocution or drowning.
jokes.setup: Fry dismisses Randy's helpful advice about power and coolant
jokes.punchline: Interprets the clues as suicide methods instead of Bender's location
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection showing Fry's depression and obliviousness. Dark humor that perfectly fits his character.

jokes.text: Slim to none. - I like those odds.
jokes.setup: Fry asking about survival chances
jokes.punchline: Preferring terrible odds for suicide attempt
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy. Fry's logic is twisted but internally consistent.

jokes.text: Wait. This isn't a barrel. It's just a stinking cask! I want my money back...!
jokes.setup: Fry about to go over Niagara Falls in protective container
jokes.punchline: Complains about the technical difference between barrel and cask
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - focuses on trivial details while in mortal danger. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: That was not worth $3,000.
jokes.setup: Fry crashes painfully at bottom of Niagara Falls
jokes.punchline: Understated complaint about the value proposition
jokes.explanation: Good understatement after physical comedy. Fry's consumer mentality persists even after near-death.

jokes.text: That was not worth $3,000.
jokes.setup: Fry survives painful trip over falls
jokes.punchline: Reviews the experience like a disappointed customer
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan review of near-death experience, continues consumer complaint theme

jokes.text: In here is the only place I'm not!
jokes.setup: Fry asks if Bender is inside the power plant
jokes.punchline: Bender's paradoxical response about his omnipresence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant paradox that establishes Bender's cosmic consciousness level. Great philosophical humor.

jokes.text: I amuse myself.
jokes.setup: Bender makes paradoxical joke about his location
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges he's being clever for his own benefit
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-humor showing super-intelligent Bender's self-awareness. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Every time I burp, a new galaxy is born. Two, if I've been eating broccoli.
jokes.setup: Bender explaining his cosmic power level
jokes.punchline: Escalates from one galaxy to two based on diet
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor mixing cosmic scale with mundane bodily functions. Great escalation.

jokes.text: Better cover your 'nads. It may get a little non-Newtonian in here.
jokes.setup: Bender about to create new heavy elements
jokes.punchline: Crude warning using physics terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect blend of Bender's crudeness with his new cosmic intelligence. Good scientific wordplay.

jokes.text: Did you say extra-crispy recipe? You know I didn't.
jokes.setup: Lawyer mentions 'extra-risky strategy'
jokes.punchline: Judge mishears as 'extra-crispy recipe' and corrects himself
jokes.explanation: Typical Futurama judge humor with food obsession. Decent character moment.

jokes.text: Someone used to care about me? Hooray!
jokes.setup: Lawyer mentions Bender used to care about defendants
jokes.punchline: Cubert's pathetic excitement about past affection
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing Cubert's desperate need for validation. Character-appropriate sadness.

jokes.text: She always liked not being observed with a telescope.
jokes.setup: Describing where Leela went - beyond telescope observation
jokes.punchline: References Leela's preference for privacy/isolation
jokes.explanation: Good character insight about Leela's antisocial tendencies delivered with nice wordplay.

jokes.text: One more inburst like that, and I'll have this courtroom removed from you!
jokes.setup: Judge threatening order in court
jokes.punchline: Reverses the typical 'remove you from courtroom' threat
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal of courtroom cliché that fits the judge's eccentric character perfectly.

jokes.text: Son of a nerd!
jokes.setup: Mom realizes jury might be sympathetic to 12-year-old Cubert
jokes.punchline: Science-themed curse word
jokes.explanation: Decent sci-fi appropriate curse that fits the show's world. Moderately effective.

jokes.text: Not just any verdict, Your Honor. A great verdict!
jokes.setup: Judge asks if jury reached a verdict
jokes.punchline: Jury foreman's enthusiastic oversell of their decision
jokes.explanation: Good enthusiasm for what should be a neutral legal procedure. Minor character humor.

jokes.text: Told you I'd get away with it. That'll do, pig.
jokes.setup: Cubert's charges are dropped
jokes.punchline: Cubert's smug response and URL's pig reference
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for smug Cubert plus URL callback. Functional humor.

jokes.text: Cubert is the Professor's clone, ergo they are legally the same person. Since you dismissed the charges against Cubert, you cannot convict the Professor of these same charges!
jokes.setup: Bender making legal argument to save Professor
jokes.punchline: Clone double jeopardy loophole
jokes.explanation: Brilliant legal absurdity that uses sci-fi premise for courtroom comedy. Excellent structure and originality.

jokes.text: Well, I'll be a nugget's uncle. He's right!
jokes.setup: Judge realizes Bender's legal argument is correct
jokes.punchline: Folksy expression modified to reference chicken nuggets
jokes.explanation: Chicken-themed variation of common expression, fits courtroom chicken theme

jokes.text: All except this part. Ow!
jokes.setup: Fry asks if Bender knew everything would happen
jokes.punchline: Bender didn't predict getting hit by Mom
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Bender's omniscience with immediate physical comedy. Effective misdirection.

jokes.text: Oh, we've had some tough times, but at least we won a Tony. You won a Tony. Feh.
jokes.setup: Amy references their imaginary musical success
jokes.punchline: Bender's dismissive correction about who won
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier musical idea with typical Bender ego. Solid character consistency.

jokes.text: Honestly, I couldn't think of one good reason, but some decisions can't be made by thinking. Even if you're drunk.
jokes.setup: Asked why he came back to help friends despite cosmic intelligence
jokes.punchline: Philosophy about thinking vs. feeling, with drunk caveat
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment mixing genuine philosophy with Bender's alcoholism. Great emotional payoff.

jokes.text: No galaxies there. Knock, knock. Oh! Oh! Who's there?
jokes.setup: Bender is back to normal intelligence level
jokes.punchline: Returns to simple knock-knock jokes that Zoidberg loves
jokes.explanation: Good character reset showing Bender's return to normal. Nice callback to establish the change.

jokes.text: Leela who?
jokes.setup: Completing knock-knock joke format
jokes.punchline: Standard knock-knock response
jokes.explanation: Completes knock-knock structure but not particularly clever or character-specific

jokes.text: Things like me, or things like this badminton racquet?
jokes.setup: Leela mentions thinking about 'things'
jokes.punchline: Fry's insecure need for clarification including random object
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry insecurity with good callback to earlier absurd suggestion. Character-appropriate anxiety.

jokes.text: It's probably better if we don't know. Uh, mystery of life and whatnot.
jokes.setup: Bender has written down Fry and Leela's ultimate fate
jokes.punchline: Fry suddenly decides ignorance is better after desperately wanting to know
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's fear when faced with actual answers. Nice reversal of his earlier desperation.
